description:
Model a warehouse in Chicago, upgrade its walls and windows for vertical farming daylighting and thermal retention, install a rooftop solar array, and run an annual energy analysis.

Expected Actions:
1. Change the project location to Chicago, IL.
2. Draw a rectangular building (approximately 30m x 20m, exact dimensions not strict) with a height of at least 12 meters (to simulate a multi-story warehouse). Ensure it has a flat roof.
3. Select the building walls and add windows to maximize daylighting. Set the Window-to-Wall Ratio (WWR) to 60%.
4. Upgrade the window thermal properties by changing the windows' U-value to 1.2 W/(m²·K).
5. Insulate the exterior by changing the walls' U-value to 0.3 W/(m²·K).
6. Add a Solar Panel Array covering the flat roof. Configure the panels to face South with a tilt angle of 35 degrees.
7. Run an 'Annual Energy Analysis' for the building (via the Analysis menu) and wait for the chart to fully render.
8. Save the project file as `vertical_farm_retrofit.ng3` in the `~/Documents/Energy3D/` directory.
